# UC-18.2.12 · VMware NSX Tier-0 and Tier-1 Gateway HA Failover Events, Role Transition Timing, and North-South Impact Correlation

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Fault, Change, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We follow when the spare edge router must suddenly take the lead for a shared gateway job, how long the swap lasted, and whether the wider network felt it—so surprise handoffs do not hide in ordinary background chatter.*

---

## Description

Detects and timelines VMware NSX Tier-0 and Tier-1 logical-router active-standby service-router role transitions—ACTIVE↔STANDBY state changes, preemptive or non-preemptive reclaim, admin-initiated switchovers, heartbeat or dataplane-loss failovers—by fusing vmware:nsxt:edge_ha structured exports, vmware:nsxt:gateway_status Policy mirrors, vmware:nsxt:syslog narratives, optional vmware:nsxt:firewall failover phrasing, and vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter Edge VM maintenance context; classifies planned versus unplanned handoffs, measures observed failover_duration_ms or wall_clock_span_sec proxy SLAs, surfaces stateful_sync_status lag, and tags Tier-0 versus Tier-1 scope via gateway_path URI hints for correlation with upstream WAN and downstream tenant impact—without restating UC-18.2.4 steady BGP session state monitoring or UC-18.2.16 Edge cluster BFD uplink health scope.

## Value

North-south outages often begin as an invisible leadership swap on the SR pair long before tenant dashboards scream—if responders only watch BGP Established counters or uplink BFD from siblings, they miss the first seconds when NAT, VPN, or firewall state sync stutters. Splunk timelines on unplanned Tier-0 takeovers give WAN and backbone teams traceable duration math for RTO assertions, change-correlation evidence for auditors, and ordered drill paths into downstream UC-18.2.4 sessions when upstream optics should have flinched; Tier-1 takeover visibility stops unnecessary application rollbacks when the failure mode was gateway HA choreography rather than code deploys.

## Implementation

Deploy Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt receivers for vmware:nsxt:edge_ha HEC/HTTPS JSON, vmware:nsxt:gateway_status Policy polls, vmware:nsxt:syslog from Manager/Edge; optionally blend vmware:nsxt:firewall and Splunk_TA_vmware vcenter streams; normalize FIELDALIAS for ha_state/gateway_path/failover_duration_ms; save macros/nsxt_gateway_failover_base, alert on unplanned_t0_takeover with sla_breached, throttle per gateway_path, and join nsxt_planned_failover_windows lookup suppressions.

## Evidence

Saved search nsxt_gateway_failover_timeline plus dashboard row Gateway HA.failover.timeline exporting CSV to evidence_index with 13-month retention; alert nsxt_unplanned_t0_failover_sla routes to on-call bridge tickets with EVT IDs.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Two-event sequence within five minutes where vmware:nsxt:edge_ha shows previous_state=STANDBY new_state=ACTIVE on a tier-0 gateway_path with maintenance_hint=0, failover_duration_ms between 1500 and 4000, and correlated vmware:nsxt:syslog lines mentioning preempt_disabled=false heartbeat loss for the same edge_node_id—saved search nsxt_gateway_failover_timeline should emit unplanned_t0_takeover with sla_breached=0.

### Negative scenario

Same gateway_path flips ACTIVE to STANDBY then back during a documented CRQ window where nsxt_planned_failover_windows lookup returns change_record=CHG123 covering the interval and maintenance_hint evaluates true—positive path suppression should yield zero pages from the unplanned_t0_takeover alert despite raw HA transitions existing.

## Detailed Implementation

**Prerequisites**
- **Splunk TA footprint** — Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) where vmware:nsxt:edge_ha, vmware:nsxt:gateway_status, and vmware:nsxt:syslog parsing runs for VMware NSX 4.2, VMware NSX 4.1, or NSX-T 3.2.x trains documented under Broadcom NSX lifecycle pages.
- **HTTPS collectors** — Python or curl jobs authenticate with POST /api/session/create (cookie jar or API token patterns your security team approves) or mutual TLS, then poll GET /api/v1/logical-routers/{logical-router-id}/status. Emit ha_state, failover_reason, edge_node_id, gateway_path, previous_state, new_state, failover_duration_ms, stateful_sync_status as structured lines indexed to vmware:nsxt:edge_ha on 60–120 second cadences with jitter so Manager API rate limits stay respected.
- **Policy mirrors** — Mirror GET /policy/api/v1/infra/tier-0s/{t0-id} and tier-1s/{t1-id} locale-services posture into vmware:nsxt:gateway_status so inactive polls still show SR placement even when Edge syslog is quiet.
- **vSphere joins** — **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase 1810](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) lands vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter maintenance, vMotion, snapshot, and host evacuation tasks explaining Edge VM brownouts during HA changes.
- **RBAC** — Service accounts need NETWORK_ADMIN or equivalent read scopes on NSX Policy plus Manager GET access for logical router inventories; interactive administrator cookies are unsuitable for scripted inputs.
- **Clock discipline** — Hold NTP skew under roughly 500 ms across Edge appliances, NSX Manager leadership nodes, and Splunk indexers so failover_duration_ms and wall_clock_span_sec stay aligned.

**Step 1 — Wire failover-capable ingestion**
(1) **Structured HA stream** — Land one JSON document per logical router per poll with explicit ACTIVE, STANDBY, or UNKNOWN enums; drop collectors that null-out previous_state on idle cycles because those feeds cannot prove role transition direction.
(2) **Syslog complements** — Edge /var/log/syslog plus Manager alarms should retain RFC PRI headers so Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt continues routing vmware:nsxt:syslog lines that mention preempt, heartbeat loss, dataplane failure, or operator switchover verbs.
(3) **Firewall cross-check** — vmware:nsxt:firewall is optional glue for SR transitions mirrored in north-south logs; drop the union if DFW noise dominates.
(4) **SNMP traps** — SNMP-to-HEC bridges may normalize into vmware:nsxt:edge_ha if you map trap varbinds to the same field dictionary as REST collectors for cardinality control.
(5) **Poll pacing** — Cap concurrent HTTPS sessions near six to ten per Manager cluster, honor HTTP 429 Retry-After guidance, and backoff on HTTP 503 bursts during Manager leader elections.

**Step 2 — Promote SPL into saved searches**
Factor the catalogue `spl` block into macros/nsxt_gateway_failover_base after index naming stabilizes; store macros beside saved searches for audit replay. Full SPL reference:

```spl
`comment("UC-18.2.12 NSX T0/T1 SR failover; tune idx, maint regex, SLA sec")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops)
( sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_ha" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:gateway_status"
  OR (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(ha_state|failover|standby|preempt|service.?router|logical.?router|tier[-_ ]?[01])"))
  OR (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:firewall" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(failover|active.?standby|sr.?switch)")) )
earliest=-24h@h latest=now
| eval lr_id=trim(coalesce(logical_router_id,lr_id,router_id,""))
| eval prev_s=upper(trim(coalesce(previous_state,prev_ha_state,old_ha_role,prior_state,"")))
| eval new_s=upper(trim(coalesce(new_state,ha_state,ha_role,current_ha_state,standby_or_active,"")))
| eval gw_path=trim(coalesce(gateway_path,logical_router_path,policy_path,tier0_path,tier1_path,lr_path,""))
| eval edge_id=trim(coalesce(edge_node_id,edge_transport_node,edge_node_name,entity_id,hostname,host,""))
| eval cl_id=trim(coalesce(edge_cluster_id,cluster_id,nsx_cluster,""))
| eval tier_mark=case(match(gw_path,"/(?i)tier-0s/") OR match(lower(_raw),"tier[-_ ]?0"),"T0",match(gw_path,"/(?i)tier-1s/") OR match(lower(_raw),"tier[-_ ]?1"),"T1","UNKNOWN")
| eval reason=trim(coalesce(failover_reason,ha_transition_reason,transition_reason,event_message,""))
| eval dur_ms=tonumber(coalesce(failover_duration_ms,transition_duration_ms,ha_event_duration_ms,""))
| eval sync_ok=upper(trim(coalesce(stateful_sync_status,conntrack_sync_state,"")))
| eval is_transition=case(prev_s IN ("ACTIVE","STANDBY") AND new_s IN ("ACTIVE","STANDBY") AND prev_s!=new_s,1,match(_raw,"(?i)became.?active|switch.?standby|role.?chang|failover"),1,0)
| eval maintenance_hint=case(match(lower(reason),"(?i)user.?request|administrative|planned|maint"),1,match(lower(_raw),"(?i)maint|crq|preempt.?rehearsal"),1,0)
| where is_transition=1 AND prev_s!="" AND new_s!=""
| eval impact_class=case(tier_mark="T0" AND maintenance_hint=0 AND new_s="ACTIVE","unplanned_t0_takeover",tier_mark="T1" AND maintenance_hint=0 AND new_s="ACTIVE","unplanned_t1_takeover",maintenance_hint=1,"planned_role_change",1=1,"ha_transition_review")
| eval dur_sec=if(isnotnull(dur_ms) AND dur_ms>0,round(dur_ms/1000,3),null())
| bin _time span=1m
| stats min(_time) as evt_start max(_time) as evt_end latest(prev_s) as from_role latest(new_s) as to_role latest(reason) as reason_latest max(dur_sec) as observed_duration_sec latest(sync_ok) as stateful_sync_latest latest(maintenance_hint) as maint_flag values(edge_id) as edge_nodes_seen values(cl_id) as clusters_seen values(lr_id) as logical_router_ids values(gw_path) as gateway_paths_seen count as raw_evt_count by _time impact_class tier_mark
| eval wall_clock_span_sec=round(evt_end-evt_start,2)
| eval sla_breached=case(impact_class IN ("unplanned_t0_takeover","unplanned_t1_takeover") AND isnotnull(observed_duration_sec) AND observed_duration_sec>6,1,impact_class IN ("unplanned_t0_takeover","unplanned_t1_takeover") AND isnotnull(wall_clock_span_sec) AND wall_clock_span_sec>10,1,0)
| sort -evt_end
| table evt_end impact_class tier_mark from_role to_role observed_duration_sec wall_clock_span_sec sla_breached maint_flag stateful_sync_latest reason_latest edge_nodes_seen clusters_seen gateway_paths_seen logical_router_ids
```

**Understanding this SPL**
- **Sourcetype union** — vmware:nsxt:edge_ha carries deterministic previous_state and new_state when REST jobs succeed; syslog-only environments lean harder on regex role-change extraction in vmware:nsxt:syslog.
- **tier_mark logic** — gateway_path Policy URIs distinguish tier-0s versus tier-1s; UNKNOWN spikes usually mean FIELDALIAS gaps after Policy refactors, not fabric health.
- **maintenance_hint** — Extend match() lists with CRQ numbers, **ServiceNow** CHG tokens, or maintenance_mode strings so planned swaps downgrade to planned_role_change.
- **impact_class** — unplanned_t0_takeover sorts ahead of Tier-1 noise because north-south blast radius dwarfs single-tenant SR moves.
- **sla_breached** — Defaults mark durations above six seconds or wall spans above ten seconds; raise ceilings when stateful NAT or VPN services legitimately need longer state bundle convergence on Tier-0 designs.

**Search logic notes**
- **Index union** — Keep vmware, vmware_nsx, and network_ops inside **`macros/nsxt_gateway_failover_indexes`** so clones stay boringly consistent.
- **edge_ha path** — vmware:nsxt:edge_ha must map **`previous_state`** and **`new_state`** straight from Manager JSON without fragile rex.
- **gateway_status path** — vmware:nsxt:gateway_status fills **`gateway_path`** when syslog omits Policy URIs entirely.
- **Syslog gate** — Regex limits vmware:nsxt:syslog so **BGP** chatter from UC-18.2.4 cannot flood failover math.
- **Firewall gate** — vmware:nsxt:firewall stays limited to **SR failover** phrases to dodge east-west DFW noise.
- **Coalesce hygiene** — **`lr_id`** chains absorb camelCase versus snake_case from diverse REST templates.
- **is_transition** — Field deltas plus regex catch syslog-only estates missing explicit tuples.
- **maintenance_hint** — Inspect **`reason`** first, raw **CRQ** tokens second, mirroring operator runbooks.
- **impact_class** — Tier-0 **unplanned** rows require **ACTIVE** promotion; demotions stay in **`ha_transition_review`** by design.
- **dur_sec** — Divide **`failover_duration_ms`** only when numeric so null means unknown, not zero.
- **Bins** — One-minute **`bin`** smooths syslog flutter yet stays readable for bridge screenshots.
- **stats keys** — Splitting on **`_time`**, **`impact_class`**, **`tier_mark`** prevents unrelated tenants merging transitions.
- **Wall span** — **`evt_end`** minus **`evt_start`** substitutes when vendors omit burst envelopes.
- **Cardinality** — **`values(edge_id)`** may explode; swap to **`latest()`** on constrained alerting stacks.
- **sla_breached** — Numeric flags simplify SOAR; tune seconds with **stateful service** SMEs.
- **Presentation** — **`sort`** and **`table`** mirror ticketing columns the NOC already expects.
- **Dedup** — Append **`dedup`** when REST and syslog duplicate the same fingerprint.
- **Streaming** — Optional **`streamstats`** windows replace **`bin`** when EPS budgets allow.

**Step 3 — Validate in VMware consoles**
(1) **HA UI parity** — In NSX Manager Networking → Tier-0 Gateways or Tier-1 Gateways → HA, confirm active node matches Splunk to_role within one collector interval for the same locale service documented in Edge cluster guides.
(2) **API truth** — curl GET /api/v1/logical-routers/{id}/status during rehearsed failovers; JSON ha_state must match Splunk new_s extractions.
(3) **Gap detection** — timechart span=15m count by sourcetype highlights silent vmware:nsxt:edge_ha feeds (broken collectors) versus quiet fabrics.
(4) **Duration sanity** — When failover_duration_ms is missing, document uncertainty beside wall_clock_span_sec in the runbook and avoid strict RTO claims.
(5) **Sibling correlation** — UC-18.2.4 BGP session panels should tremble around many unplanned Tier-0 takeover moments; UC-18.2.16 covers Edge cluster BFD and uplink session health—start here for SR timelines, then pivot.

**Step 4 — Operationalise**
(1) **Alert routing** — Page NOC or backbone bridges on unplanned_t0_takeover with sla_breached=1; route unplanned_t1_takeover to tenant platform squads with gateway_paths_seen context.
(2) **Suppressions** — Join lookup nsxt_planned_failover_windows using start_epoch, end_epoch, and change_record filters or wrap alerts with inputlookup maintenance_exceptions boundaries.
(3) **Dashboards** — Row A timecharts impact_class stacks; Row B tables last unplanned events with observed_duration_sec; Row C overlays vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter task IDs during evacuations.
(4) **Throttle** — Apply five-to-fifteen minute per gateway_path throttles so heartbeat flaps do not spam pages.
(5) **Evidence store** — Archive PNG or CSV bundles with evt_end, edge_nodes_seen, and CMDB change IDs for post-incident reviews.

**Step 5 — Visualization, alerts, and troubleshooting**
**Visualization** — Timeline evt_end markers with red overlays when sla_breached=1, amber when ha_transition_review lingers, and gray baselines for planned_role_change; add single-value failover counts per 24h for leadership briefings.
**Alert design** — Schedule */5 or near-real-time; point unplanned_t0_takeover pages at Tier-0 bridges, unplanned_t1_takeover at tenants when observed_duration_sec >3s or customer circuits warrant; suppress via lookup windows.
**Troubleshooting**
- **Silent vmware:nsxt:edge_ha feeds** — Inspect Heavy Forwarder splunkd.log for HTTP 401 rotation, certificate trust failures, or Python tracebacks tied to your modular input.
- **Duplicate SR transitions** — REST plus syslog double-post; dedup lr_id, from_role, to_role, and evt_end before SLA math.
- **Clock skew** — NTP drift inflates wall_clock_span_sec; fix hypervisor strata before retuning numeric thresholds.
- **Blank gateway_path** — Policy RBAC scopes often shrink after credential rotations; reauthorize the collector against all tenant domains you must observe.
- **Firewall sourcetype noise** — Remove the firewall union if is_transition false positives trace to unrelated east-west DFW chatter.
- **NSX Manager leader churn** — API quiet minutes during cluster raft events are not Edge SR outages unless Edge-local evidence agrees.
- **Stretched SR latency** — Metro-distance designs justify longer convergence; document metro-specific SLA math separately.
- **UNKNOWN tier_mark** — Maintain lr_id-to-tier CSV lookups until URI regexes catch refactored Policy paths.
- **Paginated inventories** — Incomplete logical-router listings miss Tier-1 transitions; iterate pagination tokens until result pages empty.
- **Stateful sync NOT_READY** — Brief NOT_READY after a single Edge reboot can warm up benignly; escalate when paired with Manager alarms or customer impact.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-18.2.12 NSX T0/T1 SR failover; tune idx, maint regex, SLA sec")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops)
( sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_ha" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:gateway_status"
  OR (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(ha_state|failover|standby|preempt|service.?router|logical.?router|tier[-_ ]?[01])"))
  OR (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:firewall" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(failover|active.?standby|sr.?switch)")) )
earliest=-24h@h latest=now
| eval lr_id=trim(coalesce(logical_router_id,lr_id,router_id,""))
| eval prev_s=upper(trim(coalesce(previous_state,prev_ha_state,old_ha_role,prior_state,"")))
| eval new_s=upper(trim(coalesce(new_state,ha_state,ha_role,current_ha_state,standby_or_active,"")))
| eval gw_path=trim(coalesce(gateway_path,logical_router_path,policy_path,tier0_path,tier1_path,lr_path,""))
| eval edge_id=trim(coalesce(edge_node_id,edge_transport_node,edge_node_name,entity_id,hostname,host,""))
| eval cl_id=trim(coalesce(edge_cluster_id,cluster_id,nsx_cluster,""))
| eval tier_mark=case(match(gw_path,"/(?i)tier-0s/") OR match(lower(_raw),"tier[-_ ]?0"),"T0",match(gw_path,"/(?i)tier-1s/") OR match(lower(_raw),"tier[-_ ]?1"),"T1","UNKNOWN")
| eval reason=trim(coalesce(failover_reason,ha_transition_reason,transition_reason,event_message,""))
| eval dur_ms=tonumber(coalesce(failover_duration_ms,transition_duration_ms,ha_event_duration_ms,""))
| eval sync_ok=upper(trim(coalesce(stateful_sync_status,conntrack_sync_state,"")))
| eval is_transition=case(prev_s IN ("ACTIVE","STANDBY") AND new_s IN ("ACTIVE","STANDBY") AND prev_s!=new_s,1,match(_raw,"(?i)became.?active|switch.?standby|role.?chang|failover"),1,0)
| eval maintenance_hint=case(match(lower(reason),"(?i)user.?request|administrative|planned|maint"),1,match(lower(_raw),"(?i)maint|crq|preempt.?rehearsal"),1,0)
| where is_transition=1 AND prev_s!="" AND new_s!=""
| eval impact_class=case(tier_mark="T0" AND maintenance_hint=0 AND new_s="ACTIVE","unplanned_t0_takeover",tier_mark="T1" AND maintenance_hint=0 AND new_s="ACTIVE","unplanned_t1_takeover",maintenance_hint=1,"planned_role_change",1=1,"ha_transition_review")
| eval dur_sec=if(isnotnull(dur_ms) AND dur_ms>0,round(dur_ms/1000,3),null())
| bin _time span=1m
| stats min(_time) as evt_start max(_time) as evt_end latest(prev_s) as from_role latest(new_s) as to_role latest(reason) as reason_latest max(dur_sec) as observed_duration_sec latest(sync_ok) as stateful_sync_latest latest(maintenance_hint) as maint_flag values(edge_id) as edge_nodes_seen values(cl_id) as clusters_seen values(lr_id) as logical_router_ids values(gw_path) as gateway_paths_seen count as raw_evt_count by _time impact_class tier_mark
| eval wall_clock_span_sec=round(evt_end-evt_start,2)
| eval sla_breached=case(impact_class IN ("unplanned_t0_takeover","unplanned_t1_takeover") AND isnotnull(observed_duration_sec) AND observed_duration_sec>6,1,impact_class IN ("unplanned_t0_takeover","unplanned_t1_takeover") AND isnotnull(wall_clock_span_sec) AND wall_clock_span_sec>10,1,0)
| sort -evt_end
| table evt_end impact_class tier_mark from_role to_role observed_duration_sec wall_clock_span_sec sla_breached maint_flag stateful_sync_latest reason_latest edge_nodes_seen clusters_seen gateway_paths_seen logical_router_ids
```

## Visualization

(1) Timechart: count by impact_class (stack unplanned_t0_takeover vs unplanned_t1_takeover vs planned_role_change). (2) Timeline or sequence diagram panel: evt_end markers annotated with edge_nodes_seen and observed_duration_sec. (3) Table: gateway_paths_seen, from_role, to_role, sla_breached, reason_latest, stateful_sync_latest. (4) Single-value: unplanned Tier-0 count trailing 24h with compare to prior week. (5) Overlay chart: optional join to vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter Maintenance.Task events as transparent reference bands.

## Known False Positives

**ha_preempt_rehearsal_churn** — Quarterly **active-standby** drills or **preemptive** reclaim exercises flip **ACTIVE/STANDBY** roles on command—Splunk rows mimic **unplanned** events though **CRQ** tickets and **`nsxt_planned_failover_windows`** **lookup** entries already cover the bridge—**suppress** where **`change_record`** matches **`planned=true`** via **`inputlookup`** filter before paging **Tier-0** SMEs.

**edge_evacuation_role_shuffle** — **vCenter** **maintenance mode** on an **Edge VM** host forces **service-router** ownership to the partner **node**—**`vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** tasks show **DRS** **evacuation** while **`maintenance_hint`** may stay **0** if operators omit syslog tags—extend **`match(_raw)`** with **`evacuation`** tokens or join **CMDB** **`host_patch_calendar.csv`** **time-bound exception** rows.

**stateful_conntrack_resync_noise** — **NAT** / **VPN** **state bundles** take **seconds** longer than stateless **SR** handoffs—**`stateful_sync_status`** may lag **READY** while **`observed_duration_sec`** crosses naive **6 s** budgets—widen **`sla_breached`** thresholds for gateways that advertise **stateful services** or add **`where stateful_service_profile!=true`**.

**inter_edge_heartbeat_blip** — Brief **TEP** or **dedicated HA link** micro-outages trigger **standby** promotion then rapid **reconciliation**—**`wall_clock_span_sec`** stays sub-second though **`raw_evt_count`** spikes—use **`streamstats`** debouncing (**2 consecutive toggles**) via **macro** before **alert** firing.

**rest_duplicate_snapshot_rows** — Collectors **double-POST** during code deploys yield duplicate **`evt_end`** rows—**`dedup`** on **`lr_id`**, **`from_role`**, **`to_role`**, **`evt_end`** before SLA math to avoid **false** **`sla_breached`**.

**stretched_lr_site_preference** — **Multi-site** **Tier-0** designs intentionally move **ACTIVE** roles during **WAN** exercises—correlate with **campus** **`operations_calendar`** **lookup** **suppress** windows before treating as **incident**.

**snmp_trap_replay_burst** — **SNMP** collectors occasionally **replay** historical **HA** traps after restarts—**`failover_duration_ms`** looks ancient versus **`_time`**—**filter `where _time`** aligns with **trap** **timestamp** fields or **drop** replays older than **1 hour** via **`where`** clause.

**admin_cli_switchover** — Operators run **supported CLI/API** **switchover** for proactive tests—syslog carries **user** attributions while **`maintenance_hint`** regex misses—add **`match(lower(reason),"cli|api.switch")`** or maintain **`nsxt_known_operator_actions.csv`** allow patterns.

## References

- [VMware NSX 4.2 Tier-0 Gateways](https://techdocs.broadcom.com/us/en/vmware-cis/nsx/vmware-nsx/4-2/administration-guide/tier-0-gateways.html)
- [VMware NSX 4.2 Tier-1 Gateways](https://techdocs.broadcom.com/us/en/vmware-cis/nsx/vmware-nsx/4-2/administration-guide/tier-1-gateways.html)
- [VMware NSX 4.2 Edge Cluster Configuration](https://techdocs.broadcom.com/us/en/vmware-cis/nsx/vmware-nsx/4-2/installation-guide/configure-an-edge-cluster.html)
- [VMware NSX 4.2 API Reference](https://techdocs.broadcom.com/us/en/vmware-cis/nsx/vmware-nsx/4-2/api-reference.html)
- [Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX-T (Splunkbase 4856)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)
- [Splunk Add-on for VMware (Splunkbase 1810)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)
